reasoning: Based on historical analogies for large-scale US jail escapes (5+ escapees), most are apprehended quickly, but a 'long tail' often ensues for the remaining few. At least 3 were apprehended within the first 3 days, but 7 were still at large as of May 19. Statistically, fugitives who evade the initial 72-hour dragnet have often successfully fled the immediate area or secured reliable safehouses, transitioning to a slower, more difficult phase of the manhunt. A mixture model assuming 80% of escapees are disorganized (half-life of a few days) and 20% are more capable/lucky (half-life of ~30 days) suggests that 1 or 2 out of the original 10 might take months to capture. Given the strong initial progress but the large total number of remaining targets, I estimate roughly a 45% chance that *all* 10 are completely rounded up by the July 1, 2025 deadline (46 days post-escape).
